headline: Hornworm check today, blackberry tomorrow, 85°F Tuesday
weather_notes: Hold all watering today — Rachio ran 2 days ago at 75°F with zero rain risk; every active bed is at adequate moisture.
Water potato_bags and c2–c4 bean beds by Tuesday — 85°F heat arrives with no rain in the 7-day forecast; bag soil and just-sown bean seeds lose moisture faster than established raised beds.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today — tomato beds are 22 days in and entering the peak hornworm window; today's warm sunny conditions are ideal for checking lower leaves for frass before the week heats up.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check blackberry for flower clusters
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow — Triple Crown is entering its first fruiting season at year 2 and confirming flowers now sets expectations for summer berry yield.

suggested_tasks.title: Check frost-damaged potato sprouts for recovery
suggested_tasks.description: Two sprouts were knocked back by frost May 23 — 8 days later with 85°F arriving Tuesday, confirm recovery or spot-replant before soil temperatures spike.
